Meta.Title: User Flow Improvement Plan
Meta.Note: No product changes have been implemented yet. This document is for planning purposes only.

Section.Title: User Flow Issues (Feedback Incorporated)
Section.Note: The items below capture reported issues in the current product experience. Proposed changes are documented in relevant sections later in this plan.

Issue.Title: Onboarding front-loads abstract concepts
Issue.Details: Steps 3 (aspirational roles) and 4 (gravitational pull) introduce nuanced concepts before the user has experienced the app at all. Users are being asked to reflect on their relationship with each role before they've logged a single minute of life. These steps would land better after a first week of use, when the data makes them meaningful.
Issue.WhatsBroken: Cognitive overload before value is demonstrated.

Issue.Title: Dashboard empty state has no clear first action
Issue.Details: After onboarding, the user lands on a blank dashboard. The empty state says "Your flow starts with a single moment. What are you doing right now?" — but the + button is at the bottom of the screen in the nav bar. There's no visual arrow, no inline CTA, nothing connecting the prompt to the action. The FocusModeIndicator says "Not focused" which means nothing to a first-time user.
Issue.WhatsBroken: The onboarding exits but doesn't hand off to a clear next step.

Issue.Title: Activity templates are built but never surfaced
Issue.Details: Templates exist in state, can be created in Profile settings, and ship with 4 defaults. The QuickAdd modal never shows them. The Calendar page never shows them. They are completely invisible during the actual logging flow.
Issue.WhatsBroken: A time-saving feature that users will never discover.

Issue.Title: Focus Mode and activity logging feel disconnected
Issue.Details: A user can set their focus to "Work" via the FocusModeIndicator, but nothing then prompts them to log activities for Work. The ambient background tint changes, but there's no feedback loop: "You've been focused on Work for 45 minutes — want to log it?" The focus state and the activity log are two separate islands.
Issue.WhatsBroken: The two most important interactions don't reinforce each other.

Issue.Title: Tapping a role replaces the entire app
Issue.Details: In App.tsx, tapping a role block sets viewingRoleId, which unmounts the whole main layout and renders RoleOverviewPage instead. There's no navigation stack — the user just loses context. From RoleOverviewPage they can only delete activities or tap Back. They can't log a new activity for that role, edit the role, or return to where they were in the time-period filter they had selected.
Issue.WhatsBroken: Navigation is destructive and the role detail page offers no useful actions.

Issue.Title: ActivityLogPage.tsx exists but is never rendered
Issue.Details: There's a full activity log page written and not wired into the app anywhere. The dashboard shows a maximum of 5 activities. There's no way for a user to see their full history.
Issue.WhatsBroken: A core piece of functionality is unreachable.

Issue.Title: Profile tab is a feature dumping ground
Issue.Details: Role Management, Balance Goals, Transition Rituals, Activity Templates, and Data & Privacy are all behind accordion sections in a single tab. A new user would never discover Balance Goals or Templates — they require actively exploring a Settings screen. These are not settings; they're core features of the app.
Issue.WhatsBroken: Feature discoverability. New users will use 20% of what the app offers.

Issue.Title: Reflection tab is entirely passive
Issue.Details: There's no prompt, nudge, or indicator anywhere in the app that connects to the Reflect tab. No "You haven't reflected this week" badge on the nav icon. No completion state visible from other screens. The reflection feature requires the user to remember it exists and proactively navigate to it.
Issue.WhatsBroken: The feature has no trigger. Most users will forget it's there after day two.

Issue.Title: No role editing after creation
Issue.Details: In Profile, roles can be paused or deleted but not edited. A user who misspells their role name or wants to change the emoji has to delete the role — which cascades and removes all its activity associations — and start over.
Issue.WhatsBroken: A basic CRUD operation is missing, with destructive consequences.

Section.Summary: The onboarding experience is overloaded and cognitively heavy.

Issue.Description: Users are presented with too much information and too many decisions during onboarding, which can feel overwhelming and may cause drop-off.

Onboarding steps that rely on nuanced, reflective concepts (e.g., aspirational roles, gravitational pull) are introduced before users have logged any data, increasing cognitive load before value is demonstrated.
When users are asked about their roles, they are not given any suggestions or examples, forcing them to come up with roles from scratch.

Improvement.Approach: Break onboarding into clearer, smaller steps, and defer non-essential questions until the user has experienced core value (e.g., capturing first activities).
Improvement.Notes: Consider a progress indicator and contextual help/tooltips instead of long upfront explanations.

Improvement.Goal: Make nuanced concepts meaningful by introducing them after usage data exists.
Improvement.Approach: Move steps like "aspirational roles" and "gravitational pull" out of first-run onboarding. Re-introduce them after ~1 week of activity logging, when the user has enough data/context to reflect productively.

Improvement.Goal: Help users define roles quickly and accurately during onboarding.
Improvement.Approach: Introduce role suggestions when asking for roles, such as: "Friend", "Parent", "Partner", "Professional", "Leader", "Learner", "Artist", "Caregiver", "Community Member", "Athlete".

ImplementationIdeas.Idea: Display suggested roles as selectable chips/pills that can be tapped to add.
Allow free-text entry in addition to suggestions for flexibility.
Use short example descriptions for a few roles to clarify what a "role" means.

Section.Summary: The post-onboarding landing experience needs a clear first action and better handoff.

Issue.Description: The dashboard empty state prompt is not visually connected to the primary logging action (+), and first-time users don't understand status labels like "Not focused".

Improvement.Goal: Make the first activity log unmistakable.
Improvement.Approach: Add an inline primary CTA within the empty state (e.g., "Log what you're doing" button) that triggers the same flow as the + button. Visually link the prompt to the action (layout, arrow, or button placement).

Improvement.Goal: Make focus state legible to a brand-new user.
Improvement.Approach: Replace "Not focused" with first-run-friendly copy (e.g., "Pick a focus to guide your next log") and/or hide focus status until the user has used focus at least once.

Section.Summary: Activity templates exist but are not discoverable in the logging flow.

Issue.Description: Templates can be created and default templates exist, but templates are not surfaced in the QuickAdd modal or Calendar, making the feature effectively undiscoverable.

Improvement.Goal: Make templates a first-class, time-saving path to logging.
Improvement.Approach: Show templates in QuickAdd (e.g., a "Templates" section with the default templates and user-created ones), and allow one-tap logging from a template.

Improvement.Goal: Make templates discoverable in another high-frequency logging surface.
Improvement.Approach: Add templates to the Calendar page (e.g., quick chips above the day view) to enable rapid logging without searching.

Section.Summary: Focus Mode and activity logging should reinforce each other.

Issue.Description: Setting focus changes ambient UI but does not create a prompt, shortcut, or feedback loop into activity logging for that role.

Improvement.Goal: Create a loop between focus and logging.
Improvement.Approach: After a configurable duration focused on a role (e.g., 30–60 minutes), show a lightweight nudge like "You've been focused on Work for 45 minutes — log it?" that opens QuickAdd prefilled with the focused role and suggested duration.

Improvement.Goal: Reduce friction when logging while focused.
Improvement.Approach: When focus is set, default the role selector in the logging flow to the focused role and optionally highlight relevant templates for that role.

Section.Summary: The main page currently shows roles as rectangles, which is not meaningful.

Issue.Description: Roles are displayed in a flat, rectangular format that does not convey how they relate to each other or to the user's overall life balance.

Improvement.Goal: Represent roles as a holistic view of the user's life.
Improvement.Approach: Replace the rectangular role layout with a donut chart where each segment represents a role, visually making up the user's life.

Details.Detail: Each role becomes a segment in a donut chart, with color-coding per role.
Segment size can represent time spent, importance, or a configurable metric.
Hover/tap on a segment shows quick stats: recent activities, percentage of time, and energizing vs non-energizing balance.
Consider a central label/title that reflects the overall life view (e.g., "Your Roles" or a short insight).

RisksAndQuestions.Item: Need to decide what data determines segment size (equal vs weighted).
Accessibility considerations for color-blind users and screen readers.

Section.Summary: Role navigation should preserve context and role pages should offer useful actions.

Issue.Description: Tapping a role replaces the entire app surface (no navigation stack), causing loss of context and limiting actions within the role detail experience.
Role overview currently limits users to viewing/deleting activities and going Back; it does not support common next actions like logging a new activity for the role or editing the role.

Improvement.Goal: Preserve app context when drilling into a role.
Improvement.Approach: Use a proper navigation pattern (route, stack, or modal sheet) so role details are navigated to without unmounting the entire main layout. Preserve filters/time period state on return.
Add primary actions on role overview such as "Log activity" (prefilled to the role), "Edit role", and quick filters (e.g., energizing only).

Section.Summary: Users need a way to access their full activity history beyond the dashboard preview.

Issue.Description: The dashboard shows only a handful of activities and there is no user path to a full activity log/history view, even though a full activity log page exists.

Improvement.Approach: Add navigation entry points to the full activity log (e.g., "See all" on dashboard recent activity, a dedicated tab, or a route accessible from profile or calendar).

Section.Summary: Core features should be discoverable and not hidden behind a settings-style profile screen.

Issue.Description: The Profile tab currently acts as a dumping ground for core features (roles, goals, rituals, templates, privacy), reducing discoverability and making high-value features feel optional.
Reflection is passive and requires user memory; there are no nudges, badges, or completion cues elsewhere in the app to pull users into reflection.

Improvement.Approach: Promote key features (Templates, Balance Goals, Transition Rituals) into primary navigation or contextual surfaces (e.g., logging flows, dashboard cards), leaving Profile for true settings/account/privacy.

Improvement.Goal: Give reflection a clear trigger and completion visibility.
Improvement.Approach: Add indicators and prompts such as a weekly "Reflect" nudge, a badge on the Reflect tab when pending, and a visible completion state on dashboard or role insights.

Section.Summary: Users should be able to edit roles after creation without destructive consequences.

Issue.Description: Roles can be paused or deleted but not edited, forcing destructive workflows for simple fixes like typos or emoji changes.

Improvement.Approach: Add role editing (name, emoji, color, etc.) without requiring deletion, preserving activity associations.

Section.Summary: Users can mark activities as energizing and view them in a role overview page.

Behavior.Description: When logging an activity, there is an optional toggle to indicate whether the activity felt energizing.
In the role overview page, users can see the activities that felt energizing for that role.

Addition.Goal: Encourage consistent use of the energizing toggle without making it mandatory.
Addition.Approach: Make the energizing toggle more noticeable (e.g., microcopy or subtle animation) and explain briefly why marking energizing activities matters.

Addition.Goal: Connect energizing activities to the new visual representation of roles.
Addition.Approach: Link energizing activity data to the donut visualization and role overview:

Details.Detail: Show an energizing vs non-energizing ratio per role when a donut segment is selected.
Surface a short insight such as "Most energizing activities recently come from your <Role> role."
Provide a quick shortcut from the donut or main page into the role overview filtered to energizing activities.

Addition.Goal: Help users identify patterns in what energizes them over time.
Addition.Approach: In future iterations, consider simple trend views (e.g., weekly count of energizing activities per role) within the role overview or a dedicated insights area.

NextSteps.Step: Validate this plan with stakeholders and adjust priorities if needed.
Break down each improvement into design tasks (wireframes, prototypes) and technical tasks before implementing any changes.
Prototype the updated onboarding role selection with suggestions and test with a small group of users.
Explore visual design options for the roles-as-donut main page concept, including accessibility considerations.
Define data requirements and tracking for energizing activities to support both the role overview and donut insights.
Design the post-onboarding dashboard empty state handoff (inline CTA, first-run copy) and validate that users reliably complete their first log.
Decide where templates should live in the logging flow (QuickAdd, Calendar, role pages) and design the template surfacing strategy.
Define the focus-to-logging feedback loop (nudge timing, copy, dismissal rules) and test for usefulness vs annoyance.
Replace destructive role navigation with a preserved-context navigation pattern and add primary role actions (log, edit, filters).
Wire the full activity log/history page into navigation and add clear entry points from the dashboard.
Rework information architecture so core features are discoverable (reduce reliance on Profile accordions; add reflection triggers and completion cues).
Add role editing (non-destructive CRUD) to prevent data loss and reduce friction.
